content.Text:
[Wed 2026-04-22 16:11 PDT] Add a "Smart Burnout Detector" feature to this Flutter app. This is an autonomous burnout risk analyzer that monitors multiple wellness signals and provides proactive recovery recommendations.

Create these 2 files:

## 1. lib/core/services/burnout_detector_service.dart

A service that:
- Defines `BurnoutRiskLevel` enum: low, moderate, elevated, high, critical (with labels, emojis, colors)
- Defines `BurnoutSignal` class with: name, category (sleep, mood, energy, activity, social, nutrition), value (0-100), weight, trend (improving/stable/declining)
- Defines `BurnoutAnalysis` class with: overallRisk, riskScore (0-100), signals list, recommendations list, recoveryPlan (list of steps), warningPatterns (detected patterns), resilience score
- `BurnoutDetectorService` class with:
  - `generateSampleSignals()` → returns realistic sample data with 12+ signals across categories (sleep quality, sleep duration, mood stability, energy level, exercise frequency, social interaction, screen time, work hours, break frequency, hydration, nutrition quality, mindfulness minutes)
  - `analyzeSignals(List<BurnoutSignal> signals)` → returns BurnoutAnalysis with weighted composite score, pattern detection, risk classification
  - `detectPatterns(signals)` → finds concerning patterns like "sleep declining while work hours increasing", "social isolation pattern", "energy crash cycle"
  - `generateRecommendations(analysis)` → context-aware recommendations based on which signals are worst
  - `generateRecoveryPlan(analysis)` → phased recovery: immediate (today), short-term (this week), medium-term (this month)
  - `calculateResilience(signals)` → resilience score based on positive signals that buffer against burnout
  - `getSampleScenarios()` → returns 4 named scenarios: "Healthy Balance", "Early Warning", "Approaching Burnout", "Critical Burnout" - each with different signal profiles

## 2. lib/views/home/burnout_detector_screen.dart

A full Flutter screen with:
- AppBar: "🔥 Burnout Detector" with scenario dropdown button
- Top section: Large circular risk gauge showing overall score (0-100) with color coding and risk level label
- Resilience badge showing resilience score
- Signal cards in a grid: each signal shows name, value bar (color-coded), trend arrow (↑↗→↘↓), category icon
- Warning patterns section: expandable cards showing detected patterns with severity
- Recommendations section: numbered actionable items with priority icons
- Recovery plan section: 3-phase collapsible plan (Immediate/Short-term/Medium-term)
- Scenario switcher: buttons to load different scenarios to see how risk changes
- Uses sample data by default (like GoalAutopilotScreen pattern)

Follow the exact same code patterns as the existing screens in this project (look at lib/views/home/goal_autopilot_screen.dart and lib/core/services/goal_autopilot_service.dart for reference). Use the same import style, widget structure, and theming approach.

## 3. Register in command palette

Edit lib/core/services/command_palette_service.dart:
- Add an entry in the buildActions() method in the "AI & Insights" or "Smart" category section. Add it near other smart/autonomous tools. Use pattern:
  `_nav('nav_burnout', 'Burnout Detector', Icons.local_fire_department, 'Smart', ['burnout', 'stress', 'wellness', 'health', 'risk'])`

Edit lib/views/widgets/command_palette_overlay.dart:
- Add import: `import '../home/burnout_detector_screen.dart';`
- Add route in _screenRoutes map: `'nav_burnout': () => BurnoutDetectorScreen(),`

## Build verification
After making all changes, run: `dart analyze --no-fatal-infos lib/core/services/burnout_detector_service.dart lib/views/home/burnout_detector_screen.dart`
If dart analyze isn't available, at minimum run: `dart compile kernel --no-link-platform lib/core/services/burnout_detector_service.dart` to check syntax.

DO NOT create any new branches or PRs. Just make changes on the current branch.
I’m inspecting the existing goal autopilot screen/service and the command palette wiring first so the new burnout feature matches the project’s patterns instead of introducing a new style.

I have the reference pattern. Next I’m checking whether there are other “smart” screens or reusable UI conventions in this app that the new detector should mirror before I write the new service and screen.

`rg` isn’t installed here, so I’m falling back to targeted file reads. I found several adjacent “insight” screens; I’m checking those to align naming, cards, and expansion patterns with the rest of the app.

I have enough context. I’m adding a service-first model layer with typed patterns, recommendations, and phased recovery data so the screen can stay simple and match the project’s card/expansion style. After that I’ll wire the palette and run the requested Dart check.I’m at the edit step now. I’m creating the service and screen together so the UI can bind against the final typed analysis model in one pass, then I’ll make the small palette wiring changes.
